891dbe7 2026-03-30 fix(web): allow microphone access + widen CSP for ElevenLabs and LiveKit - Permissions-Policy: microphone=() → microphone=(self) so browser
  mic prompt works for voice input
- connect-src: add api.elevenlabs.io and *.livekit.cloud (wss + https)
  so TTS streaming and LiveKit room connections are not blocked

b46975a 2026-03-30 docs(release): add live deployment link to RELEASE.md front door Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

3aa3dbc 2026-03-29 fix(web): install Tailwind v4 and unblock Google Fonts in CSP - Add tailwindcss@^4 — all omega/ components use Tailwind classes that
  were silently no-ops without it, causing the unstyled layout
- Add @import "tailwindcss" to globals.css (Tailwind v4 / Turbopack style)
- Extend CSP font-src and style-src to allow fonts.googleapis.com and
  fonts.gstatic.com so Orbitron and Space Grotesk actually load

f38225a 2026-03-29 feat(web): add full-stack OmegA chat UI with hooks and components - Replaces redirect-only page.tsx with complete chat interface
- Adds use-omega-chat hook: streaming /api/chat, conversation management, telemetry, runtime-status polling
- Adds use-voice wrapper around existing useVoiceEngine
- Adds use-mobile hook for responsive breakpoint
- Adds 9 omega/ components: OmegaHeader, ChatView, ChatComposer, ConversationSidebar, TelemetryPanel, VoiceMode, SystemStateDrawer, SettingsModal, ActionSheet
- Adds omega-cosmic-bg CSS utility class to globals.css

1	# SOP: OmegA Database Expansion
2	**Version:** 1.0
3	**Author:** Claude (Lead Architect)
4	**Applies to:** All CLI agents — Claude, , Codex, Aider, and any future agents
5	**Trigger:** Neon DB project reaches 90% — auto-prune triggered. Expansion at 99% (~507 MB of 512 MB free tier)
6	
7	---
8	
9	## The Core Principle
10	
11	OmegA runs on free infrastructure only. Neon's free tier gives 512 MB per project. When a DB fills up, **we do not upgrade — we expand horizontally** by creating a new free Neon project and routing new namespaces to it. All projects stay linked at the ingest and query layers.
12	
13	This is the permanent solution. Never pay. Always chain.
14	
15	---
16	
17	## Current DB Registry
18	
19	| DB | Project Name | Endpoint | Capacity | Namespaces |
20	|---|---|---|---|---|
21	| DB1 | omega-personal-db | `ep-sweet-glade-anvm0pwn` | **446 MB / 512 MB (87.3%) — NO NEW WRITES** | `personal`, `social_*`, `omega_brain`, `fresh_downloads*`, `home_full`, historical namespaces |
22	| DB2 | omega-technical-db | `ep-snowy-dew-ak5hrcr3` | **49 MB / 512 MB (9.6%) — DEFAULT_DB** | `omega_architecture`, `omega_docs`, `omega_code`, `system`, `skill_bundle`, `claude_skills`, `agent_skills`, `cli_agents`, `google_takeout`, `omega_sovereign`, `aeon_system`, `documents`, `safa_data`, `inbox` + all new namespaces |
23	
24	**Connection strings are in:** `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/VAULT/one-true.env`
25	```
26	OMEGA_DB_URL   = DB1 (personal)
27	OMEGA_DB2_URL  = DB2 (technical)
28	OMEGA_DB3_URL  = DB3 (when created — see procedure below)
29	```
30	
31	---
32	
33	## Trigger: When to Expand
34	
35	**Any agent** that runs an ingest script or queries the DB should check storage before starting work:
36	
37	```python
38	import psycopg2
39	
40	def check_db_health(uri, label):
41	    conn = psycopg2.connect(uri)
42	    cur = conn.cursor()
43	    cur.execute("SELECT pg_database_size('neondb')")
44	    size_bytes = cur.fetchone()[0]
45	    pct = (size_bytes / (512 * 1024 * 1024)) * 100
46	    cur.close(); conn.close()
47	    print(f"{label}: {size_bytes / 1024 / 1024:.1f} MB ({pct:.1f}%)")
48	    return pct
49	
50	# Prune at 90%, expand at 99%
51	for label, uri in DB_REGISTRY.items():
52	    if check_db_health(uri, label) >= 90:
53	        print(f"⚠️  {label} at {pct:.1f}% — auto-prune. Expand at 99%.")
54	```
55	
56	**Thresholds: 90% = auto-prune, 99% = expand.** At 99%, stop ingesting to that DB and follow the procedure below.
57	
58	---
59	
60	## Expansion Procedure
61	
62	### Step 1 — Create a New Free Neon Project
63	
64	Use the Neon MCP tool (available to Claude) or the Neon CLI:
65	
66	```bash
67	# Via Neon CLI (free, open source)
68	neonctl projects create --name "omega-db3-<theme>"
69	# Example themes: conversations, archive, research, agents
70	```
71	
72	Or ask Claude: *"Create a new Neon project called omega-db3"* — Claude has the Neon MCP and will do it instantly.
73	
74	**Record the connection string.** It will look like:
75	```
76	postgresql://neondb_owner:<password>@ep-<slug>-pooler.<region>.aws.neon.tech/neondb?sslmode=require
77	```
78	
79	---
80	
81	### Step 2 — Enable pgvector and Create the Schema
82	
83	Run this against the new DB immediately after creation:
84	
85	```python
86	import psycopg2
87	
88	NEW_DB_URI = "postgresql://..."  # from step 1
89	
90	conn = psycopg2.connect(NEW_DB_URI)
91	cur = conn.cursor()
92	cur.execute("CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector")
93	cur.execute("""
94	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.omega_memory_entries (
95	    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
96	    content TEXT,
97	    embedding vector(384),
98	    source TEXT,
99	    namespace TEXT,
100	    domain TEXT,
101	    importance FLOAT,
102	    created_at TEXT,
103	    version INTEGER,
104	    confidence FLOAT
105	)
106	""")
107	conn.commit()
108	cur.close(); conn.close()
109	print("New DB ready.")
110	```
111	
112	---
113	
114	### Step 3 — Save to Vault
115	
116	Append the new connection string to `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/VAULT/one-true.env`:
117	
118	```bash
119	echo "OMEGA_DB3_URL='postgresql://...'" >> /home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/VAULT/one-true.env
120	```
121	
122	---
123	
124	### Step 4 — Assign Namespaces
125	
126	Decide which namespaces the new DB owns. Follow the pattern:
127	
128	| DB# | Theme | Namespaces |
129	|---|---|---|
130	| DB1 | Personal | biography, conversations, social_* |
131	| DB2 | Technical | canon, code, docs, skills, system |
132	| DB3 | Next overflow | assign whatever is filling fastest |
133	
134	**Rule:** Never split a namespace across two DBs. A namespace lives in exactly one DB.
135	
136	---
137	
138	### Step 5 — Update the DB Registry
139	
140	Update this file (`SOP_DATABASE_EXPANSION.md`) — add the new DB to the **Current DB Registry** table at the top.
141	
142	Also update `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/INGEST/db_registry.py` (the shared registry all ingest scripts import):
143	
144	```python
145	# Add to DB_REGISTRY dict
146	DB_REGISTRY['db3'] = {
147	    'uri': 'postgresql://...',
148	    'namespaces': {'new_namespace_1', 'new_namespace_2'},
149	    'label': 'omega-db3-theme'
150	}
151	```
152	
153	---
154	
155	### Step 6 — Update Ingest Scripts
156	
157	In any ingest script that uses a hardcoded `DB_URI`, switch to importing from the registry:
158	
159	```python
160	from db_registry import get_conn_for_namespace
161	
162	# Instead of:
163	conn = psycopg2.connect(DB_URI)
164	
165	# Do:
166	conn = get_conn_for_namespace(namespace)
167	```
168	
169	---
170	
171	### Step 7 — Update the Query Layer
172	
173	Any script or API endpoint that queries OmegA memory must fan out to ALL registered DBs and merge results:
174	
175	```python
176	from db_registry import DB_REGISTRY
177	
178	def query_all_dbs(embedding, top_k=10):
179	    results = []
180	    for db_info in DB_REGISTRY.values():
181	        conn = psycopg2.connect(db_info['uri'])
182	        cur = conn.cursor()
183	        cur.execute("""
184	            SELECT content, source, namespace, importance,
185	                   1 - (embedding <=> %s::vector) AS similarity
186	            FROM public.omega_memory_entries
187	            ORDER BY embedding <=> %s::vector
188	            LIMIT %s
189	        """, (embedding, embedding, top_k))
190	        results.extend(cur.fetchall())
191	        cur.close(); conn.close()
192	    # Sort merged results by similarity
193	    results.sort(key=lambda x: x[4], reverse=True)
194	    return results[:top_k]
195	```
196	
197	---
198	
199	### Step 8 — Log to ERGON.md
200	
201	After completing expansion, log it to The Block:
202	
203	```
204	[TIMESTAMP] [AGENT] DB expansion: created omega-db3. Assigned namespaces: X, Y. Total capacity now: N GB free.
205	```
206	
207	---
208	
209	## Storage Optimization (Before Expanding)
210	
211	Before creating a new DB, always try pruning first:
212	
213	```python
214	# Delete entries below importance threshold to reclaim space
215	cur.execute("DELETE FROM public.omega_memory_entries WHERE importance <= 0.5")
216	conn.commit()
217	conn.autocommit = True
218	cur.execute("VACUUM FULL public.omega_memory_entries")
219	```
220	
221	If pruning recovers enough space (drops below 90%), skip expansion. Only expand if after pruning you are still at or above 99%.
222	
223	---
224	
225	## Summary Checklist
226	
227	- [ ] DB hits 90% → auto-prune `importance <= 0.5` first
228	- [ ] Still at 99%+ after pruning → create new Neon project (free)
229	- [ ] Enable pgvector + create `omega_memory_entries` table
230	- [ ] Save URI to vault as `OMEGA_DB{N}_URL`
231	- [ ] Assign namespaces (no namespace splits across DBs)
232	- [ ] Update `db_registry.py`
233	- [ ] Update ingest scripts to use registry
234	- [ ] Update query layer to fan out to all DBs
235	- [ ] Update this SOP's DB Registry table
236	- [ ] Log to ERGON.md
237	
238	---
239	
240	## Philosophy
241	
242	Neon gives us 512 MB free per project. There is no stated limit on how many free projects you can create. We chain them indefinitely. 1 GB, 2 GB, 4 GB — all free, all linked, all transparent to OmegA's query layer. The system scales horizontally forever at zero cost.
243	
244	**This is the OmegA way: free, sovereign, self-scaling.**
245

def magenta(t): return f"\033[35;1m{t}\033[0m" if C else t

def red(t):     return f"\033[31m{t}\033[0m" if C else t

        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=120) as resp:

                if not chunk:
                    break
                buf += chunk
                while b"\n" in buf:
                    line, buf = buf.split(b"\n", 1)
                    text = line.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
                    if text.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):
                        continue
                    if text.strip():
                        print(text, end="", flush=True)
                        full += text
            if buf:
                text = buf.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
                if not text.startswith('data: {"type":"status"'):

        print(red(f"\nerror {e.code}: {body}"), file=sys.stderr)

        print(red(f"Check that {API_BASE} is reachable."), file=sys.stderr)

    return full

        import atexit
        atexit.register(lambda: readline.write_history_file(str(HISTORY_FILE)))
    except ImportError:
        pass

    print(magenta("  Ω  OmegA"))

    print(dim("  /new · clear history    /exit · quit"))
